questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Avion ends in a consonant (-n), so it is masculine (muški rod). Most nouns ending in a consonant are masculine.

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Knjiga ends in -a, so it is feminine (ženski rod). Most nouns ending in the vowel -a are feminine.

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Sunce ends in -e, so it is neuter (srednji rod). Most nouns ending in -o or -e are neuter.

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Stablo ends in -o, so it is neuter (srednji rod). Most nouns ending in -o or -e are neuter.

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Hotel ends in a consonant (-l), so it is masculine (muški rod). Most nouns ending in a consonant are masculine.

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Stolica ends in -a, so it is feminine (ženski rod). Most nouns ending in the vowel -a are feminine.

questions.prompt: What gender is the noun 'auto' (car)? Pazite!
questions.hint: This is a tricky one! The ending might fool you. Think about what 'auto' refers to.
questions.explanation: PAZITE! Auto ends in -o but is masculine (muški rod), not neuter! This is an exception. Some masculine nouns end in -o (auto, Marko, Hrvoje). Its plural is 'auti' (not 'auta').

questions.hint: Look at the ending of the word. What does it end with?
questions.explanation: Jezero ends in -o, so it is neuter (srednji rod). Most nouns ending in -o or -e are neuter.

questions.prompt: Which Croatian demonstrative pronoun means 'this' (something close to the speaker)?
questions.explanation: 'Ovo' is used for something close to the person speaking. 'To' = close to the listener. 'Ono' = far from both.

questions.prompt: Which Croatian demonstrative pronoun means 'that' (something close to the listener)?
questions.explanation: 'To' is used for something close to the person being spoken to (the listener). 'Ovo' = close to speaker. 'Ono' = far from both.

questions.prompt: Which Croatian demonstrative pronoun means 'that over there' (something far from both speaker and listener)?
questions.explanation: 'Ono' is used for something far from both the speaker and the listener. 'Ovo' = close to speaker. 'To' = close to listener.

questions.prompt: How do masculine nouns (muški rod) form their plural?
questions.explanation: Masculine nouns form the plural by adding -i: mobitel → mobiteli, prozor → prozori, hotel → hoteli.

questions.prompt: How do feminine nouns (ženski rod) form their plural?
questions.explanation: Feminine nouns form the plural by changing -a to -e: knjiga → knjige, torba → torbe, olovka → olovke.

questions.prompt: How do neuter nouns (srednji rod) form their plural?
questions.explanation: Neuter nouns form the plural by changing -o or -e to -a: računalo → računala, sunce → sunca, stablo → stabla.

questions.hint: This is an irregular plural — it doesn't follow the normal rules!
questions.explanation: PAZITE! Čovjek has an irregular plural: ljudi (people). This must be memorized as it doesn't follow the standard masculine plural rule.

questions.hint: Masculine nouns form the plural by adding -i.
questions.explanation: Mobitel is masculine. Add -i: mobitel → mobiteli.

questions.hint: Feminine nouns form the plural by changing -a to -e.
questions.explanation: Knjiga is feminine. Change -a to -e: knjiga → knjige.

questions.hint: Neuter nouns form the plural by changing -o/-e to -a.
questions.explanation: Računalo is neuter. Change -o to -a: računalo → računala.

questions.hint: Neuter nouns form the plural by changing -o/-e to -a.
questions.explanation: Sunce is neuter. Change -e to -a: sunce → sunca.

questions.hint: Feminine nouns form the plural by changing -a to -e.
questions.explanation: Torba is feminine. Change -a to -e: torba → torbe.

questions.hint: Masculine nouns form the plural by adding -i.
questions.explanation: Prozor is masculine. Add -i: prozor → prozori.

questions.hint: Masculine nouns form the plural by adding -i.
questions.explanation: Autobus is masculine. Add -i: autobus → autobusi.

questions.hint: Feminine nouns form the plural by changing -a to -e.
questions.explanation: Olovka is feminine. Change -a to -e: olovka → olovke.

questions.hint: Neuter nouns form the plural by changing -o/-e to -a.
questions.explanation: Stablo is neuter. Change -o to -a: stablo → stabla.

questions.prompt: What is the plural of 'auto'? Pazite!
questions.hint: Auto is masculine despite ending in -o! Masculine plurals add -i, so the -o changes to -i.
questions.explanation: PAZITE! Auto is masculine (not neuter). Its plural follows the masculine rule: auto → auti. The -o ending is replaced with -i.

questions.hint: Feminine nouns form the plural by changing -a to -e.
questions.explanation: Stolica is feminine. Change -a to -e: stolica → stolice.

questions.hint: Masculine nouns form the plural by adding -i.
questions.explanation: Poklon is masculine. Add -i: poklon → pokloni.
Torba (f.) = bag. 'Ovo je' is used with singular nouns: Ovo je torba.

questions.hint: Knjiga (f.) → plural?
questions.explanation: Knjiga (f.) = book. Plural: knjige. With plural nouns, use 'su' instead of 'je': Ovo su knjige.
Stablo (n.) = tree. 'Ovo je' is used with singular nouns: Ovo je stablo.

questions.hint: Prozor (m.) → plural?
questions.explanation: Prozor (m.) = window. Plural: prozori. With plural nouns, use 'su': Ovo su prozori.
Poklon (m.) = gift. 'Ovo je' is used with singular nouns: Ovo je poklon.

questions.hint: Računalo (n.) → plural?
questions.explanation: Računalo (n.) = computer. Plural: računala. With plural nouns, use 'su': Ovo su računala.
Sladoled (m.) = ice cream. 'Ovo je' is used with singular nouns: Ovo je sladoled.

questions.hint: Olovka (f.) → plural?
questions.explanation: Olovka (f.) = pencil. Plural: olovke. With plural nouns, use 'su': Ovo su olovke.
Ovo = this. Je = is. Knjiga (f.) = book. 'Ovo je' introduces a singular object.
Ovo su = these are. Mobiteli = phones (m. pl., from mobitel). 'Ovo su' introduces plural objects.
Ovo = this. Je = is. Sunce (n.) = sun.
Ovo su = these are. Stolice = chairs (f. pl., from stolica).
Ovo = this. Je = is. Avion (m.) = airplane.
Ovo su = these are. Torbe = bags (f. pl., from torba).

questions.hint: Window = prozor. Use 'Ovo je' for singular nouns.
questions.explanation: Prozor (m.) = window. 'Ovo je' introduces a singular object: Ovo je prozor.

questions.hint: Book = knjiga. Use 'Ovo su' for plural nouns. Feminine plural: -a → -e.
questions.explanation: Knjige (f. pl.) = books. 'Ovo su' introduces plural objects: Ovo su knjige.

questions.hint: Hotel is the same word in Croatian! Use 'Ovo je' for singular.
questions.explanation: Hotel (m.) = hotel. Same word in Croatian: Ovo je hotel.

questions.hint: Tree = stablo. Use 'Ovo su' for plural. Neuter plural: -o → -a.
questions.explanation: Stabla (n. pl.) = trees. Neuter plural changes -o to -a: stablo → stabla. Ovo su stabla.

questions.hint: Suitcase = kofer. Use 'Ovo je' for singular.
questions.explanation: Kofer (m.) = suitcase. Ovo je kofer.

questions.hint: Gift = poklon. Use 'Ovo su' for plural. Masculine plural: add -i.
questions.explanation: Pokloni (m. pl.) = gifts. Masculine plural adds -i: poklon → pokloni. Ovo su pokloni.
